channel.title: NPR Topics: Education
channel.description: We've been to school. We know how education works. Right? In fact, many aspects of learning — in homes, at schools, at work and elsewhere — are evolving rapidly, along with our understanding of learning. Join us as we explore how learning happens.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Teachers across California are considering work stoppages
item.description: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.content:encoded: Teachers across California have been preparing for potential strikes as state funding for schools struggles to keep up with costs.
item.dc:creator: Farida Romero

item.title: March (Audio) Madness! Here are the finalists in NPR's College Podcast Challenge
item.description: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: From 75 campuses across 35 states, we've listened to hundreds of student entries to select the very best for NPR's College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Steve Drummond

item.title: Announcing the 2025 NPR College Podcast Challenge Honorable Mentions
item.description: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.content:encoded: Here are some of the best entries in NPR's 2025 College Podcast Challenge.
item.dc:creator: Student Podcast Challenge

item.title: College students, professors are making their own AI rules. They don't always agree
item.description: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.content:encoded: More than three years after ChatGPT debuted, AI has become a part of everyday life — and professors and students are still figuring out how or if they should use it.
item.dc:creator: Lee V. Gaines

item.title: A U.S. scholarship thrills a teacher in India. Then came the soul-crushing questions
item.description: She was thrilled to become the first teacher from a government-sponsored school in India to get a Fulbright exchange award to learn from U.S. schools. People asked two questions that clouded her joy.
item.content:encoded:
She was thrilled to become the first teacher from a government-sponsored school in India to get a Fulbright exchange award to learn from U.S. schools. People asked two questions that clouded her joy.

(Image credit: Anupam Gangopadhyay)
item.dc:creator: Joyeeta Banerjee

item.title: Columbia student detained by ICE is abruptly released after Mamdani meets with Trump
item.description: Hours after the student was taken into custody in her campus apartment, she was released, after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani expressed concerns about the arrest to President Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Hours after the student was taken into custody in her campus apartment, she was released, after New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani expressed concerns about the arrest to President Trump.

(Image credit: Seth Wenig)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Harvard professor Larry Summers to resign as school investigates his ties to Epstein
item.description: Harvard professor and economist Larry Summers will resign at the end of the academic year amid the school's on-going investigation into his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded: Harvard professor and economist Larry Summers will resign at the end of the academic year amid the school's on-going investigation into his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.
item.dc:creator: Suevon Lee

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's financial ties to higher education raises questions and protests
item.description: The recent release of files related to Jeffrey Epstein revealed his ties to several colleges and universities, raising questions and protests about how institutions should handle problematic donors.
item.content:encoded: The recent release of files related to Jeffrey Epstein revealed his ties to several colleges and universities, raising questions and protests about how institutions should handle problematic donors.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Kansas City high school teaches financial literacy with student-run credit union
item.description: Students at a Kansas City high school are learning real-life banking skills by running their own credit union in the school.
item.content:encoded: Students at a Kansas City high school are learning real-life banking skills by running their own credit union in the school.
item.dc:creator: Jodi Fortino

item.title: Facing a mental health crisis, an NJ school pulled a beloved novel from English class
item.description: Junot Díaz's The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao was removed from an English class at the public school. PEN America says it's part of a trend of scrubbing literature dealing with uncomfortable topics.
item.content:encoded:
The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao from the curriculum of a high-level English class at Columbia High School in Maplewood, N.J., in response to a mental health crisis.'/>

Junot Díaz's The Brief Wondrous Life of Oscar Wao was removed from an English class at the public school. PEN America says it's part of a trend of scrubbing literature dealing with uncomfortable topics.

(Image credit: Anastasia Tsioulcas)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas
